littledan where do I update meeting contributions with PDF of the notes?
content.formatted_body: littledan where do I update meeting contributions with PDF of the notes?

content.body: Aha, found it
portal -> meeting -> more options -> contribute to meeting
or not.
You were right. You create the contribution, then you upload it.
While the portal is painful to use, they have surprisingly good documentation. https://help.etsi.org/index.php?title=Contributions_application
Aha it's two steps, in different places. Thanks Sam
content.formatted_body: Aha it's two steps, in different places. Thanks Sam

content.body: Don't forget your cover sheet :-p
I've been assuming it's fine to forget the cover sheet

By the way, if we need someone to submit comments to national bodies, Simon is happy to do so, and can provide contacts elsewhere as well, if we provide the comment sheet from vendors that we want to address.
content.formatted_body: By the way, if we need someone to submit comments to national bodies, Simon is happy to do so, and can provide contacts elsewhere as well, if we provide the comment sheet from vendors that we want to address.

content.body: dietrich: let's keep pushing the meeting notes to this folder as well: https://forge.etsi.org/rep/cyber/stan4cr2/en-304-617/-/tree/main/Meeting%20Notes
content.formatted_body: dietrich: let's keep pushing the meeting notes to this folder as well: https://forge.etsi.org/rep/cyber/stan4cr2/en-304-617/-/tree/main/Meeting%20Notes

content.body: you can just land it directly, no need to do a PR
oh really? how does this work?
I think he’s on the UK BSI mirror committee. Just so you know there is a way.
content.formatted_body: I think he’s on the UK BSI mirror committee. Just so you know there is a way.

content.body: (I'm also reaching out to Simon)
this is really good news
thank you for asking

Simon explains that all technical feedback will need to be discussed in mirror committees, and the committee will need to decide, on a fine-grained basis, whether they agree with each little part, before sending it on
we will be able to raise issues but it's not a simple pass-through
we have plenty of overlap with DIN too
maybe smaller national bodies would operate differently

once we have a full first draft, then we will be able to decide, as a group, whether to send it on to public enquiry right away, or whether to spend more time reviewing it via people filing labs issues ("open consultation") and generally us just staring at it longer and improving things. It's not something we need to decide now.
Andrew Whalley (Google): Simon points to Martin Chapman on the Irish national body. Do you know him, or would you like an invite?
content.formatted_body: Andrew Whalley (Google): Simon points to Martin Chapman on the Irish national body. Do you know him, or would you like an invite?
